Now the white diamond is at the bottom (dark gray diamond's position) and the dark gray diamond is at the purple square area. Maybe I need to move the white diamond back up the trail to the purple ring?
Let me try clicking on the purple square's magenta center (56, 27) again to see if it moves the white diamond (now at the bottom) up the trail.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click on the purple square's magenta center at (56, 27)
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 56, 'col': 27}])
print(f"Action result: {last_action_result}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print(f"\nKey objects after action:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'p', 'M', 'G'] and n['pixels'] <= 25:
        print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check ASCII around key area
ascii_grid = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Area (rows 51-62, cols 24-32) ===")
for r in range(51, min(63, len(ascii_grid))):
    print(f"Row {r}: {ascii_grid[r][24:33]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
